revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''八剣神社'''（'''やつるぎじんじゃ'''）は[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[県社]]。[[八千矛神]]・[[日本武...'
revision.text:
'''八剣神社'''（'''やつるぎじんじゃ'''）は[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[県社]]。[[八千矛神]]・[[日本武尊]]・[[誉田別尊]]の三柱の神を祀る。[[諏訪大社]]上社の摂社である。 == 由緒 == 当社は元々、[[諏訪湖]]中高島の里に鎮座していたが、[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[高島城]]築城に際し、現在地に遷座した。[[江戸時代]]には[[高島藩]][[諏訪家]]が居城鎮護の神として崇敬し、[[明治]]以降は小和田村の産土神として庶民からの信仰を集め現在に至る。 == 諏訪湖御神渡神事 == 当社の特殊神事として、'''御神渡神事'''（'''おみわたりしんじ'''）がある。「御渡り拝観の神事」として一般に知られているこの神事は、当社の[[神職]]によって行われる。（[[諏訪湖#御神渡|御神渡]]も参照のこと）
諏訪湖で御神渡が観測されると、当社では臨時の総代会を開き、拝観式の日を決める。その後、宮司以下神職、および氏子総代は、自宅の門戸に[[注連縄]]を張り、精進潔斎に入る。拝観式当日は、まず社殿で修祓を行い、諏訪湖へ向かう。現地では二組に分かれて湖を横断し、一之御神渡・二之御神渡・佐久之御神渡を拝観して下座と上座の湖岸地点の検分を行なう。そして、社殿へ戻り、御神渡の状況を祭神へ報告する「奉告祭」が行われる。引き続き、その状況により当年の農作物の作柄、世の中の吉凶、気候等の年占が行われる。御神渡の状況と年占の結果は「注進状」に纏まられ、後日、宮司と氏子総代が[[諏訪大社]]上社へ出向いて「御神渡注進式」を行い、神前に捧げられる。これを受けて諏訪大社は、この内容を宮内庁と気象庁へ報告する恒例となっている。（かつては幕府に奉告されていた）
直近では[[2008年]]にこの神事が行われ、1月30日御神渡を観測・臨時総代会、2月2日拝観式、2月24日注進式の日程で行われた。
なお、「奉告祭」と「注進式」は御神渡の観測されない年でも行われ、「御神渡は無かった」旨の報告がなされる。特に、御神渡の無かった年の諏訪湖は「明けの海」と呼ばれる。 == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪大社]] *[[諏訪湖]] [[Category:長野県の神社]]

'''八剣神社'''（'''やつるぎじんじゃ'''）は[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]にある[[神社]]。旧社格は[[県社]]。[[八千矛神]]・[[日本武尊]]・[[誉田別尊]]の三柱の神を祀る。[[諏訪大社]]上社の摂社である。 == 由緒 == 当社は元々、[[諏訪湖]]中高島の里に鎮座していたが、[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[高島城]]築城に際し、現在地に遷座した。[[江戸時代]]には[[高島藩]][[諏訪氏|諏訪家]]が居城鎮護の神として崇敬し、[[明治]]以降は小和田村の産土神として庶民からの信仰を集め現在に至る。 == 諏訪湖御神渡神事 == 当社の特殊神事として、'''御神渡神事'''（'''おみわたりしんじ'''）がある。「御神渡り拝観の神事」として一般に知られているこの神事は、当社の[[神職]]によって行われる。（[[諏訪湖#御神渡|御神渡]]も参照のこと）
諏訪湖で御神渡が観測されると、当社では臨時の総代会を開き、拝観式の日を決める。その後、宮司以下神職、および氏子総代は、自宅の門戸に[[注連縄]]を張り、精進潔斎に入る。拝観式当日は、まず社殿で修祓を行い、諏訪湖へ向かう。現地では二組に分かれて湖を横断し、一之御神渡・二之御神渡・佐久之御神渡を拝観して下座と上座の湖岸地点の検分を行なう。そして、社殿へ戻り、御神渡の状況を祭神へ報告する「奉告祭」が行われる。引き続き、その状況により当年の農作物の作柄、世の中の吉凶、気候等の年占が行われる。御神渡の状況と年占の結果は「注進状」に纏まられ、後日、宮司と氏子総代が[[諏訪大社]]上社へ出向いて「御神渡注進式」を行い、神前に捧げられる。これを受けて諏訪大社は、この内容を宮内庁と気象庁へ報告する恒例となっている。（かつては幕府に奉告されていた）
直近では[[2008年]]にこの神事が行われ、1月30日御神渡を観測・臨時総代会、2月2日拝観式、2月24日注進式の日程で行われた。
なお、「奉告祭」と「注進式」は御神渡の観測されない年でも行われ、「御神渡は無かった」旨の報告がなされる。特に、御神渡の無かった年の諏訪湖は「明けの海」と呼ばれる。 == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪大社]] *[[諏訪湖]] [[Category:長野県の神社]]

revision.comment: 八剣神社を八剱神社に訂正。御神渡り(おみわたり)を御渡(みわたり)に訂正。直近の祭事年月日を変更

諏訪湖で御渡が観測されると、当社では臨時の総代会を開き、拝観式の日を決める。その後、宮司以下神職、および氏子総代は、自宅の門戸に[[注連縄]]を張り、精進潔斎に入る。拝観式当日は、まず社殿で修祓を行い、諏訪湖へ向かう。現地では湖周を周り、一之御渡・二之御渡・佐久之御渡を拝観して下座と上座の湖岸地点の検分を行なう。そして、社殿へ戻り、御渡の状況を祭神へ報告する「奉告祭」が行われる。引き続き、その状況により当年の農作物の作柄、世の中の吉凶、気候等の年占が行われる。御渡の状況と年占の結果は「注進状」に纏められ、後日、宮司と氏子総代が[[諏訪大社]]上社へ出向いて「御渡注進式」を行い、神前に捧げられる。これを受けて諏訪大社は、この内容を宮内庁と気象庁へ報告する恒例となっている。（かつては[[江戸幕府|幕府]]に奉告されていた）
直近では[[2013年]]にこの神事が行われ、1月22日御渡を観測・臨時総代会、1月25日拝観式、2月17日注進式の日程で行われた。
なお、「奉告祭」と「注進式」は御渡の観測されない年でも行われ、「御渡は無かった」旨の報告がなされる。特に、御渡の無かった年の諏訪湖は「明けの海」と呼ばれる。 == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪大社]] *[[諏訪湖]] {{DEFAULTSORT:やつるきしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:諏訪市]] [[Category:摂末社]]

[[誉田別尊]]| 社格=[[諏訪大社]]上社摂社| 例祭=[[7月5日]]
[[12月5日]]| }} '''八剱神社'''（やつるぎじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。[[大国主|八千矛神]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]・[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]の三柱の神を祀る。[[諏訪大社]]上社の摂社である。 == 由緒 == 当社は元々、[[諏訪湖]]中高島の里に鎮座していたが、[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[高島城]]築城に際し、現在地に遷座した。[[江戸時代]]には[[高島藩]][[諏訪氏|諏訪家]]が居城鎮護の神として崇敬し、[[明治]]以降は小和田村の産土神として庶民からの信仰を集め現在に至る。 == 諏訪湖御渡神事 == 当社の特殊神事として、'''御渡神事'''（'''みわたりしんじ'''）がある。「御渡り拝観の神事」として一般に知られているこの神事は、当社の[[神職]]によって行われる。（[[諏訪湖#御神渡|御神渡]]も参照のこと）
諏訪湖で御渡が観測されると、当社では臨時の総代会を開き、拝観式の日を決める。その後、宮司以下神職、および氏子総代は、自宅の門戸に[[注連縄]]を張り、精進潔斎に入る。拝観式当日は、まず社殿で修祓を行い、諏訪湖へ向かう。現地では湖周を周り、一之御渡・二之御渡・佐久之御渡を拝観して下座と上座の湖岸地点の検分を行なう。そして、社殿へ戻り、御渡の状況を祭神へ報告する「奉告祭」が行われる。引き続き、その状況により当年の農作物の作柄、世の中の吉凶、気候等の年占が行われる。御渡の状況と年占の結果は「注進状」に纏められ、後日、宮司と氏子総代が[[諏訪大社]]上社へ出向いて「御渡注進式」を行い、神前に捧げられる。これを受けて諏訪大社は、この内容を宮内庁と気象庁へ報告する恒例となっている。（かつては[[江戸幕府|幕府]]に奉告されていた）
直近では[[2013年]]にこの神事が行われ、1月22日御渡を観測・臨時総代会、1月25日拝観式、2月17日注進式の日程で行われた。
なお、「奉告祭」と「注進式」は御渡の観測されない年でも行われ、「御渡は無かった」旨の報告がなされる。特に、御渡の無かった年の諏訪湖は「明けの海」と呼ばれる。 == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪大社]] *[[諏訪湖]] {{DEFAULTSORT:やつるきしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:諏訪市の歴史]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:諏訪藩]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]]

諏訪湖で御渡が観測されると、当社では臨時の総代会を開き、拝観式の日を決める。その後、宮司以下神職、および氏子総代は、自宅の門戸に[[注連縄]]を張り、精進潔斎に入る。拝観式当日は、まず社殿で修祓を行い、諏訪湖へ向かう。現地では湖周を周り、一之御渡・二之御渡・佐久之御渡を拝観して下座と上座の湖岸地点の検分を行なう。そして、社殿へ戻り、御渡の状況を祭神へ報告する「奉告祭」が行われる。引き続き、その状況により当年の農作物の作柄、世の中の吉凶、気候等の年占が行われる。御渡の状況と年占の結果は「注進状」に纏められ、後日、宮司と氏子総代が[[諏訪大社]]上社へ出向いて「御渡注進式」を行い、神前に捧げられる。これを受けて諏訪大社は、この内容を宮内庁と気象庁へ報告する恒例となっている。（かつては[[江戸幕府|幕府]]に奉告されていた）
直近では[[2013年]]にこの神事が行われ、1月22日御渡を観測・臨時総代会、1月25日拝観式、2月17日注進式の日程で行われた。
なお、「奉告祭」と「注進式」は御渡の観測されない年でも行われ、「御渡は無かった」旨の報告がなされる。特に、御渡の無かった年の諏訪湖は「明けの海」と呼ばれる。 == 参考文献 == *[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』2000年 [[白水社]] == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪大社]] *[[諏訪湖]] {{DEFAULTSORT:やつるきしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:諏訪市の歴史]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:諏訪藩]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] [[Category:諏訪市の建築物]]

[[日本武尊]]
[[誉田別尊]] | 社格=[[諏訪大社]]上社摂社 | 例祭=[[7月5日]]
[[12月5日]] }} '''八剱神社'''（やつるぎじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。[[大国主|八千矛神]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]・[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]の三柱の神を祀る。[[諏訪大社]]上社の摂社である。 == 由緒 == 当社は元々、[[諏訪湖]]中にある高島の里当時の高島村のこと。社があったのは現在の高島城がある場所に鎮座し、[[諏訪湖]]の造船銭で造営されてきた。[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[高島城]]築城に際し、現在地に遷座した。[[江戸時代]]には[[諏訪藩|高島藩]][[諏訪氏|諏訪家]]が居城鎮護の神として崇敬し、[[明治]]以降は小和田村の産土神として庶民からの信仰を集め現在に至る。 == 諏訪湖御渡神事 == 当社の特殊神事として、'''御渡神事'''（'''みわたりしんじ'''）がある。「御渡り拝観の神事」として一般に知られているこの神事は、当社の[[神職]]によって行われる。（[[諏訪湖#御神渡|御神渡]]も参照のこと）

[[日本武尊]]
[[誉田別尊]] | 社格=[[諏訪大社]]上社摂社 | 例祭=[[7月5日]]
[[12月5日]] }} '''八剱神社'''（やつるぎじんじゃ）は[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。[[大国主|八千矛神]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]・[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]の三柱の神を祀る。[[諏訪大社]]上社の摂社である。 == 由緒 == 当社は元々、[[諏訪湖]]中にある高島の里当時の高島村のこと。社があったのは現在の高島城址の場所に鎮座し、[[諏訪湖]]の造船銭で造営されてきた。[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[高島城]]築城に際し、現在地に遷座した。[[江戸時代]]には[[諏訪藩|高島藩]][[諏訪氏|諏訪家]]が居城鎮護の神として崇敬し、[[明治]]以降は小和田村の産土神として庶民からの信仰を集め現在に至る。 == 諏訪湖御渡神事 == 当社の特殊神事として、'''御渡神事'''（'''みわたりしんじ'''）がある。「御渡り拝観の神事」として一般に知られているこの神事は、当社の[[神職]]によって行われる。（[[諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡]]も参照のこと）
諏訪湖で御渡が観測されると、当社では臨時の総代会を開き、拝観式の日を決める。その後、宮司以下神職、および氏子総代は、自宅の門戸に[[注連縄]]を張り、精進潔斎に入る。拝観式当日は、まず社殿で修祓を行い、諏訪湖へ向かう。現地では湖周を周り、一之御渡・二之御渡・佐久之御渡を拝観して下座と上座の湖岸地点の検分を行なう。そして、社殿へ戻り、御渡の状況を祭神へ報告する「奉告祭」が行われる。引き続き、その状況により当年の農作物の作柄、世の中の吉凶、気候等の年占が行われる。御渡の状況と年占の結果は「注進状」に纏められ、後日、宮司と氏子総代が[[諏訪大社]]上社へ出向いて「御渡注進式」を行い、神前に捧げられる。これを受けて諏訪大社は、この内容を宮内庁と気象庁へ報告する恒例となっているかつては[[江戸幕府|幕府]]に奉告されていた。 直近では[[2013年]]にこの神事が行われ、1月22日御渡を観測・臨時総代会、1月25日拝観式、2月17日注進式の日程で行われた。なお、「奉告祭」と「注進式」は御渡の観測されない年でも行われ、「御渡は無かった」旨の報告がなされる。特に、御渡の無かった年の諏訪湖は「明けの海」と呼ばれる。 == 注釈 == == 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』2000年 [[白水社]] == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪大社]] *[[諏訪湖]] {{DEFAULTSORT:やつるきしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:諏訪市の歴史]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:諏訪藩]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] [[Category:諏訪市の建築物]]

[[誉田別尊]] | 社格=[[諏訪大社]]上社摂社 | 例祭=[[7月5日]]

諏訪湖で御渡が観測されると、当社では臨時の[[総代]]会を開き、拝観式の日を決める。その後、宮司以下神職、および氏子総代は、自宅の門戸に[[注連縄]]を張り、精進潔斎[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%B2%BE%E9%80%B2%E6%BD%94%E6%96%8E-532147 コトバンク・精進潔斎]に入る。拝観式当日は、まず社殿で修[[祓]]を行い、諏訪湖へ向かう。現地では湖周を周り、一之御渡・二之御渡・佐久之御渡を拝観して下座と上座の湖岸地点の検分を行なう。そして、社殿へ戻り、御渡の状況を[[祭神]]へ報告する「奉告祭」が行われる。引き続き、その状況により当年の農作物の作柄、世の中の吉凶、気候等の年占が行われる。御渡の状況と年占の結果は「[[注進]]状」に纏められ、後日、[[宮司]]と[[氏子総代]]が[[諏訪大社]]上社へ出向いて「御渡注進式」を行い、神前に捧げられる。これを受けて諏訪大社は、この内容を[[宮内庁]]と[[気象庁]]へ報告する恒例となっているかつては[[江戸幕府|幕府]]に奉告されていた。 直近では[[2013年]]にこの神事が行われ、1月22日御渡を観測・臨時総代会、1月25日拝観式、2月17日注進式の日程で行われた。なお、「奉告祭」と「注進式」は御渡の観測されない年でも行われ、「御渡は無かった」旨の報告がなされる。特に、御渡の無かった年の諏訪湖は「明けの海」と呼ばれる。 == 注釈 == == 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』2000年 [[白水社]] == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|諏訪市の名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事]] *[[諏訪大社]] *[[諏訪湖]] *[[手長神社]] *[[足長神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ shrine-heritager 八剱神社（諏訪市）] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ 諏訪神仏プロジェクト] {{DEFAULTSORT:やつるきしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:諏訪市の歴史]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:諏訪藩]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] [[Category:諏訪市の建築物]]

[[12月5日]] }} '''八剱神社'''（やつるぎじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。[[大国主|八千矛神]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]・[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]の三柱の神を祀る。[[諏訪大社]]上社の[[摂末社|摂社]]である。 == 由緒 == 当社は元々、[[諏訪湖]]中にある高島の里当時の高島村のこと。社があったのは現在の高島城址の場所に鎮座し、諏訪湖の造船銭で造営されてきた。[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[高島城]]築城に際し、現在地に遷座した。[[江戸時代]]には[[諏訪藩|高島藩]][[諏訪氏|諏訪家]]が居城鎮護[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%8E%AE%E8%AD%B7-569923 [[コトバンク]]・鎮護]の神として崇敬し、[[明治]]以降は小和田村の[[産土神]]として庶民からの信仰を集め現在に至る。 == 諏訪湖御渡神事 == 当社の特殊神事として、'''御渡[[神事]]'''（'''みわたりしんじ'''）がある。「御渡り拝観の神事」として一般に知られているこの神事は、当社の[[神職]]によって行われる。（[[諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡]]も参照のこと）
諏訪湖で御渡が観測されると、当社では臨時の総代会を開き、拝観式の日を決める。その後、[[宮司]]以下神職、および[[総代#氏子総代|氏子総代]]は、自宅の門戸に[[注連縄]]を張り、精進潔斎[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%B2%BE%E9%80%B2%E6%BD%94%E6%96%8E-532147 コトバンク・精進潔斎]に入る。拝観式当日は、まず社殿で修[[祓]]を行い、諏訪湖へ向かう。現地では湖周を周り、一之御渡・二之御渡・佐久之御渡を拝観して下座と上座の湖岸地点の検分を行なう。そして、社殿へ戻り、御渡の状況を[[祭神]]へ報告する「奉告祭」が行われる。引き続き、その状況により当年の農作物の作柄、世の中の吉凶、気候等の年占が行われる。御渡の状況と年占の結果は「[[注進]]状」に纏められ、後日、宮司と氏子総代が[[諏訪大社#上社|諏訪大社上社]]へ出向いて「御渡注進式」を行い、神前に捧げられる。これを受けて諏訪大社は、この内容を[[宮内庁]]と[[気象庁]]へ報告する恒例となっているかつては[[江戸幕府|幕府]]に奉告されていた。 直近では[[2013年]]にこの神事が行われ、1月22日御渡を観測・臨時総代会、1月25日拝観式、2月17日注進式の日程で行われた。なお、「奉告祭」と「注進式」は御渡の観測されない年でも行われ、「御渡は無かった」旨の報告がなされる。特に、御渡の無かった年の諏訪湖は「明けの海」と呼ばれる。 == 注釈 == == 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』2000年 [[白水社]] == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪市#名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事|諏訪市の名所・旧跡・観光スポット・祭事・催事]] *[[諏訪大社]] *[[諏訪湖]] *[[手長神社]] *[[足長神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ shrine-heritager 八剱神社（諏訪市）] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ 諏訪神仏プロジェクト] {{DEFAULTSORT:やつるきしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:諏訪市の歴史]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:諏訪藩]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] [[Category:諏訪市の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル]]

[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]
[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]] | 社格=[[諏訪大社]]上社摂社 | 例祭=[[7月5日]]
[[12月5日]] }} '''八剱神社'''（やつるぎじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。[[大国主|八千矛神]]・[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]・[[応神天皇|誉田別尊]]の三柱の神を祀る。[[諏訪大社]]上社の[[摂末社|摂社]]である。 == 由緒 == 当社は元々、[[諏訪湖]]中にある高島の里当時の高島村のこと。社があったのは現在の高島城址の場所に鎮座し、諏訪湖の造船銭で造営されてきた。[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[高島城]]築城に際し、現在地に遷座した。[[江戸時代]]には[[諏訪藩|高島藩]][[諏訪氏|諏訪家]]が居城鎮護[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E9%8E%AE%E8%AD%B7-569923 『鎮護』 - コトバンク]の神として崇敬し、[[明治]]以降は小和田村の[[産土神]]として庶民からの信仰を集め現在に至る。 == 諏訪湖御渡神事 == 当社の特殊神事として、'''御渡[[神事]]'''（'''みわたりしんじ'''）がある。「御渡り拝観の神事」として一般に知られているこの神事は、当社の[[神職]]によって行われる。（[[諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡]]も参照のこと）
諏訪湖で御渡が観測されると、当社では臨時の総代会を開き、拝観式の日を決める。その後、[[宮司]]以下神職、および[[総代#氏子総代|氏子総代]]は、自宅の門戸に[[注連縄]]を張り、精進潔斎[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%B2%BE%E9%80%B2%E6%BD%94%E6%96%8E-532147 『精進潔斎』 - コトバンク]に入る。拝観式当日は、まず社殿で修[[祓]]を行い、諏訪湖へ向かう。現地では湖周を周り、一之御渡・二之御渡・佐久之御渡を拝観して下座と上座の湖岸地点の検分を行なう。そして、社殿へ戻り、御渡の状況を[[祭神]]へ報告する「奉告祭」が行われる。引き続き、その状況により当年の農作物の作柄、世の中の吉凶、気候等の年占が行われる。御渡の状況と年占の結果は「[[注進]]状」に纏められ、後日、宮司と氏子総代が[[諏訪大社#上社|諏訪大社上社]]へ出向いて「御渡注進式」を行い、神前に捧げられる。これを受けて諏訪大社は、この内容を[[宮内庁]]と[[気象庁]]へ報告する恒例となっているかつては[[江戸幕府|幕府]]に奉告されていた。 直近では[[2013年]]にこの神事が行われ、1月22日御渡を観測・臨時総代会、1月25日拝観式、2月17日注進式の日程で行われた。なお、「奉告祭」と「注進式」は御渡の観測されない年でも行われ、「御渡は無かった」旨の報告がなされる。特に、御渡の無かった年の諏訪湖は「明けの海」と呼ばれる。 == 注釈 == == 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』2000年 [[白水社]] == 関連項目 == *[[諏訪市#神社|諏訪市の神社]] *[[諏訪大社]] *[[諏訪湖]] *[[手長神社]] *[[足長神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ shrine-heritager 八剱神社（諏訪市）] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ 諏訪神仏プロジェクト]　- [[諏訪信仰]]と[[神仏習合]]と[[神仏分離]]の歴史のプロジェクト {{DEFAULTSORT:やつるきしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:諏訪市の歴史]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:諏訪藩]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] [[Category:諏訪市の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル]]

{{神社 |名称 = 八剱神社 |画像 = [[File:八剱神社.jpg|270px|八剱神社　拝殿]] |所在地 = 長野県諏訪市小和田13-18 |位置 = {{Coord|36|02|26|N|138|07|14|E|region:JP-20_scale:30000|display=it}} |祭神 = [[大国主|八千矛神]]
[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]

This thing related to the Suwa Faith does in fact exist so an article translating the Japanese wikipedia is worthwhile at some point __NOINDEX__
==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

{{Shrine |名称 = Yatsurugi Shrine |画像 = [[File:八剱神社.jpg|270px|Yatsurugi Shrine, Worship Hall]] |所在地 = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Kowada 13-18 |位置 = {{Coord|36|02|26|N|138|07|14|E|region:JP-20_scale:30000|display=it}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} (Yachihoko no Kami)
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (Nihon Take Mikoto)
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} (Homuda Wake no Mikoto) |社格 = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |例祭 = [[July 5]]
[[December 5]] }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|ja|高島城}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's {{ill|Kannushi|ja|神職}} (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

{{Shrine |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:八剱神社.jpg|270px|Yatsurugi Shrine, Worship Hall]] |address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|02|26|N|138|07|14|E|region:JP-20_scale:30000|display=it}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} (Yachihoko no Kami)
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (Nihon Take Mikoto)
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} (Homuda Wake no Mikoto) |type = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[July 5]]
[[December 5]] }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎}}). At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

{{Shrine |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:八剱神社.jpg|270px|Yatsurugi Shrine, Worship Hall]] |address = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|02|26|N|138|07|14|E|region:JP-20_scale:30000|display=it}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} (Yachihoko no Kami)
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (Nihon Take Mikoto)
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} (Homuda Wake no Mikoto) |type = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[July 5]]
[[December 5]] }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|ja|高島城}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's {{ill|Kannushi|ja|神職}} (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

{{Shrine |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:八剱神社.jpg|270px|Yatsurugi Shrine, Worship Hall]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|02|26|N|138|07|14|E|region:JP-20_scale:30000|display=it}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} (Yachihoko no Kami)
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (Nihon Take Mikoto)
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} (Homuda Wake no Mikoto) |type = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[July 5]]
[[December 5]] }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|ja|高島城}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's {{ill|Kannushi|ja|神職}} (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

[[December 5]] }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|ja|高島城}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's {{ill|Kannushi|ja|神職}} (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:八剱神社.jpg|270px|Yatsurugi Shrine, Worship Hall]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|02|26|N|138|07|14|E|region:JP-20_scale:30000|display=it}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} (Yachihoko no Kami)
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (Nihon Take Mikoto)
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} (Homuda Wake no Mikoto) |type = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[July 5]]
[[December 5]] }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|ja|高島城}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's {{ill|Kannushi|ja|神職}} (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:八剱神社.jpg|270px|Yatsurugi Shrine, Worship Hall]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|02|26|N|138|07|14|E|region:JP-20_scale:30000|display=it}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} (Yachihoko no Kami)
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (Nihon Take Mikoto)
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} (Homuda Wake no Mikoto) |type = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[July 5]]
[[December 5]] }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|ja|高島城}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's {{ill|Kannushi|ja|神職}} (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]][[Category:dd]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:八剱神社.jpg|270px|Yatsurugi Shrine, Worship Hall]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|02|26|N|138|07|14|E|region:JP-20_scale:30000|display=it}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} (Yachihoko no Kami)
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (Nihon Take Mikoto)
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} (Homuda Wake no Mikoto) |type = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[July 5]]
[[December 5]] }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|ja|高島城}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's {{ill|Kannushi|ja|神職}} (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:八剱神社.jpg|270px|Yatsurugi Shrine, Worship Hall]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|02|26|N|138|07|14|E|region:JP-20_scale:30000|display=it}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} (Yachihoko no Kami)
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (Nihon Take Mikoto)
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} (Homuda Wake no Mikoto) |type = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[July 5]]
[[December 5]] }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|ja|高島城}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's {{ill|Kannushi|ja|神職}} (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTYatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT::Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:八剱神社.jpg|270px|Yatsurugi Shrine, Worship Hall]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|02|26|N|138|07|14|E|region:JP-20_scale:30000|display=it}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} (Yachihoko no Kami)
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (Nihon Take Mikoto)
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} (Homuda Wake no Mikoto) |type = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[July 5]]
[[December 5]] }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|ja|高島城}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's {{ill|Kannushi|ja|神職}} (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT::Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT::Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = [[File:八剱神社.jpg|270px|Yatsurugi Shrine, Worship Hall]] |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36|02|26|N|138|07|14|E|region:JP-20_scale:30000|display=it}} |deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} (Yachihoko no Kami)
{{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}} (Nihon Take Mikoto)
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} (Homuda Wake no Mikoto) |type = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[July 5]]
[[December 5]] }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|ja|諏訪市}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|ja|高島城}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|ja|諏訪氏}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's {{ill|Kannushi|ja|神職}} (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|ja|注連縄}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} and the {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency|ja|気象庁}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社}} * {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|ja|諏訪湖}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}} {{DEFAULTSORT::Yatsurugi Shrine}} [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Subordinate shrines]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa, Nagano]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[en:Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa City)]]
