[[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390596]] Wikidata name: Yatsurugi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = File:八剱神社.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]], Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.04056|N|138.12056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} |type = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[:en:July 5|July 5]]
[[:en:December 5|December 5]] |location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, it enshrines three deities: [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] as Yachihoko no Kami, [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. It serves as a sub-shrine of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]], the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's [[:en:Kannushi|Kannushi]] (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|1=Lake Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206692}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ([[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]]) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjin kessai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] and the [[:en:Japan_Meteorological_Agency|Japan Meteorological Agency]].During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[:en:2013|2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Suwa|1=Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846338}} * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinaga Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], and [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|version=100608987|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Suwa|1=Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846338}} * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinaga Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390596]] Wikidata name: Yatsurugi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = File:八剱神社.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]], Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.04056|N|138.12056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} |type = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[:en:July 5|July 5]]
[[:en:December 5|December 5]] |location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, it enshrines three deities: [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] as Yachihoko no Kami, [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. It serves as a sub-shrine of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]], the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's [[:en:Kannushi|Kannushi]] (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|1=Lake Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206692}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ([[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]]) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjin kessai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] and the [[:en:Japan_Meteorological_Agency|Japan Meteorological Agency]].During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[:en:2013|2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Suwa|1=Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846338}} * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinaga Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], and [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|version=100608987|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}}

revision.text:
[[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390596]] Wikidata name: Yatsurugi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = File:八剱神社.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]], Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.04056|N|138.12056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} |type = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[:en:July 5|July 5]]
[[:en:December 5|December 5]] |location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, it enshrines three deities: [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] as Yachihoko no Kami, [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. It serves as a sub-shrine of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]], the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's [[:en:Kannushi|Kannushi]] (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|1=Lake Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206692}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ([[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]]) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjin kessai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] and the [[:en:Japan_Meteorological_Agency|Japan Meteorological Agency]].During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[:en:2013|2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Suwa|1=Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846338}} * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinaga Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], and [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|version=100608987|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], and [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|version=100608987|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}} [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]]

[[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390596]] Wikidata name: Yatsurugi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = File:八剱神社.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]], Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.04056|N|138.12056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} |type = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[:en:July 5|July 5]]
[[:en:December 5|December 5]] |location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, it enshrines three deities: [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] as Yachihoko no Kami, [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. It serves as a sub-shrine of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]], the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's [[:en:Kannushi|Kannushi]] (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|1=Lake Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206692}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ([[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]]) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjin kessai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] and the [[:en:Japan_Meteorological_Agency|Japan Meteorological Agency]].During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[:en:2013|2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Suwa|1=Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846338}} * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinaga Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], and [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|version=100608987|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}} [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]]

In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Suwa|1=Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846338}} * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinaga Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], and [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|version=100608987|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}} [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]]

[[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390596]] Wikidata name: Yatsurugi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = File:八剱神社.jpg |location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]], Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.04056|N|138.12056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} |type = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = [[:en:July 5|July 5]]
[[:en:December 5|December 5]] |location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suwa, Nagano|Suwa, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, it enshrines three deities: [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi]] as Yachihoko no Kami, [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], and [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]. It serves as a sub-shrine of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]], the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of [[:en:Takashima Castle|Takashima Castle]] by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]], the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the [[:en:Suwa clan|Suwa clan]], lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's [[:en:Kannushi|Kannushi]] (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|1=Lake Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206692}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ([[:en:Shimenawa|Shimenawa]]) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjin kessai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] and the [[:en:Japan_Meteorological_Agency|Japan Meteorological Agency]].During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in [[:en:2013|2013]], with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Suwa|1=Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846338}} * [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] * [[:en:Lake Suwa|Lake Suwa]] * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinaga Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]], and [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|version=100608987|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}} [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]]

In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Suwa|1=Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846338}} * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinaga Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|version=100608987|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}} [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]]

[[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390596]] Wikidata name: Yatsurugi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = File:八剱神社.jpg |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa, Nagano}}, Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.04056|N|138.12056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} |type = {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = {{ill|July 5|en|July 5|WD=Q2696|lt=July 5}}
{{ill|December 5|en|December 5|WD=Q2300|lt=December 5}} |location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|en|Takashima Castle|WD=Q10856395|lt=Takashima Castle}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's [[:en:Kannushi|Kannushi]] (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|1=Lake Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206692}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|en|Shimenawa|WD=Q1887421|lt=Shimenawa}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjin kessai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} and the {{ill|Japan_Meteorological_Agency|en|Japan_Meteorological_Agency|WD=Q860935|lt=Japan Meteorological Agency}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in {{ill|2013|en|2013|WD=Q1998|lt=2013}}, with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Suwa|1=Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846338}} * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinaga Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|version=100608987|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}} [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]]

Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} and the {{ill|Japan_Meteorological_Agency|en|Japan_Meteorological_Agency|WD=Q860935|lt=Japan Meteorological Agency}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in {{ill|2013|en|2013|WD=Q1998|lt=2013}}, with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Suwa|1=Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846338}} * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinaga Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|version=100608987|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}} [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = File:八剱神社.jpg |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa, Nagano}}, Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.04056|N|138.12056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} |type = {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = {{ill|July 5|en|July 5|WD=Q2696|lt=July 5}}
{{ill|December 5|en|December 5|WD=Q2300|lt=December 5}} |location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338}} }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|en|Takashima Castle|WD=Q10856395|lt=Takashima Castle}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's [[:en:Kannushi|Kannushi]] (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|1=Lake Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206692}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|en|Shimenawa|WD=Q1887421|lt=Shimenawa}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjin kessai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} and the {{ill|Japan_Meteorological_Agency|en|Japan_Meteorological_Agency|WD=Q860935|lt=Japan Meteorological Agency}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in {{ill|2013|en|2013|WD=Q1998|lt=2013}}, with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Suwa|1=Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846338}} * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinaga Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|version=100608987|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}} [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:pages]]

During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|en|Takashima Castle|WD=Q10856395|lt=Takashima Castle|1=Takashima Castle}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan|1=Suwa clan}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's [[:en:Kannushi|Kannushi]] (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|1=Lake Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206692}}). When the "Omiwatari" phenomenon is observed on Lake Suwa, the shrine convenes an extraordinary council to determine the date for the observation ceremony. Prior to the ceremony, the chief priest and other shrine officials place sacred ropes ({{ill|Shimenawa|en|Shimenawa|WD=Q1887421|lt=Shimenawa|1=Shimenawa}}) on their household gates and undertake a period of ritual purification ({{ill|Shōjin kessai|ja|精進潔斎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjin kessai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. At the lake, the priests inspect the ice formations, including the primary (一之御渡), secondary (二之御渡), and Saku no Miwatari (佐久之御渡) crossings, as well as both lower and upper shorelines. Upon returning to the shrine, a "Report Ritual" (奉告祭) is conducted to inform the enshrined deities of the lake's conditions. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. The shrine then forwards the information to the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} and the {{ill|Japan_Meteorological_Agency|en|Japan_Meteorological_Agency|WD=Q860935|lt=Japan Meteorological Agency|1=Japan Meteorological Agency}}.During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, these reports were submitted to the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The most recent instance of this ritual occurred in {{ill|2013|en|2013|WD=Q1998|lt=2013|1=2013}}, with the observation on January 22, the ceremony on January 25, and the report submission on February 17. In years when the "Omiwatari" is not observed, the ritual still takes place, with a report stating "No crossing was observed." In such cases, Lake Suwa is referred to as "the opening sea" (明けの海). == Notes == == References == * *Temples and Shrines in Shinshu* (1981), {{ill|Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja|信濃毎日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 信濃毎日新聞|1=Shinano Mainichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386181}} * {{ill|Kenichi Tanigawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanigawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}}, ed. *The Gods of Japan: Shrines and Sacred Places, Volume 9 – Mino, Hida, and Shinano* (2000), Hakusuisha. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of shrines in Suwa|ja|諏訪市#神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of shrines in Suwa|1=Suwa, Nagano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q846338}} * {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} * {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}} * {{ill|Tenaga Shrine|ja|手長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenaga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Ashinaga Shrine|ja|足長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashinaga Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://shrineheritager.com/yatsurugi-shrine/ Shrine-Heritager: Yatsurugi Shrine (Suwa)] * [https://suwa-tabi.jp/feature/suwa-shinbutsupj/ Suwa Faith Project] - History of {{ill|Suwa Faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社|1=Suwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2370082}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}, and {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|八剣神社 (諏訪市)|version=100608987|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11390596}} [[Category:Buildings in Suwa]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Suwa]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa clan]] [[Category:Suwa Domain]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] [[ja:八剣神社 (諏訪市)]] {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yatsurugi Shrine |image = File:八剱神社.jpg |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa, Nagano|1=Suwa, Nagano}}, Kowada 13-18 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.04056|N|138.12056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} |type = {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine Sub-Shrine |festival = {{ill|July 5|en|July 5|WD=Q2696|lt=July 5|1=July 5}}
{{ill|December 5|en|December 5|WD=Q2300|lt=December 5|1=December 5}} |location = {{ill|Suwa|WD=Q846338|1=Suwa, Nagano}} }} '''Yatsurugi Shrine''' (やつるぎじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suwa, Nagano|en|Suwa, Nagano|WD=Q846338|lt=Suwa, Nagano|1=Suwa, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture. Formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, it enshrines three deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} as Yachihoko no Kami, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, and {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. It serves as a sub-shrine of the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} Upper Shrine. == History == Originally located on Takashima in {{ill|Lake Suwa|en|Lake Suwa|WD=Q1206692|lt=Lake Suwa|1=Lake Suwa}}, the shrine was historically built using funds raised through shipbuilding taxes on Lake Suwa. During the construction of {{ill|Takashima Castle|en|Takashima Castle|WD=Q10856395|lt=Takashima Castle|1=Takashima Castle}} by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, the shrine was relocated to its present location. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the {{ill|Suwa clan|en|Suwa clan|WD=Q2975376|lt=Suwa clan|1=Suwa clan}}, lords of Takashima Domain, venerated the shrine as a guardian of their castle. Since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it has been worshiped as the tutelary deity of Kowada Village. == Suwa Lake Crossing Ritual == The shrine is notable for its unique ritual, the '''Lake-Crossing Ritual''' (みわたりしんじ). Commonly referred to as the "Observation of the Lake-Crossing Ritual," it is performed by the shrine's [[:en:Kannushi|Kannushi]] (Shinto priests). (See also {{ill|Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|ja|諏訪湖#御神渡り|御神渡り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Suwa#Omiwatari|1=Lake Suwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206692}}). On the day of the ceremony, they perform a purification rite (修祓) at the shrine before heading to Lake Suwa. Based on these observations, predictions about the year’s harvest, societal fortune, and climate are made. The results are compiled into a "Report Document" (注進状) and submitted during the "Lake-Crossing Report Ceremony" (御渡注進式) at the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|lt=Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}.
